blockingIssues.description: Archived Firecrawl skill symlinks under .dev/references/skills/skills/firecrawl* still point at ../../.agents/... and are broken inside the .dev archive, leaving part of the preserved reference set unusable.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify how cleanup/archive tasks should be handled when a milestone wants to preserve Factory artifacts while mission guidance forbids moving or deleting the live .factory/ directory. The relevant worker skill should explicitly instruct workers to keep the live mission infrastructure intact and archive copies separately when required.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The repo-cleanup-artifacts review found the worker had to reconcile implementation-worker's Rust/TDD-oriented procedure with mission-worker-base guidance forbidding .factory/ moves, creating avoidable ambiguity during the cleanup task.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add archive/copy guidance for directories containing relative symlinks: copied archives should either preserve valid link targets or rewrite/resolve the symlinks so the archived reference set remains usable in its new location.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The repo-cleanup-artifacts review found eight broken firecrawl* symlinks in .dev/references/skills/skills/ because they were copied with relative targets that only worked in the original .factory/.agents layout.
